meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't collaborate. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Antigravity Template to Solve Collaboration
content.seo_description: Stop working in silos. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to enable seamless collaboration for your freelance SaaS builds.
content.answer_block: Collaboration bottlenecks kill freelance velocity. By implementing the Antigravity project template, you standardize your architecture, allowing Claude Code to act as a shared context engine. This approach creates a 'single source of truth' repository, enabling multiple agents or developers to contribute without breaking production environments or losing state sync.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank screen at 2 AM, the client wants a feature update, but your local environment is a mess and your documentation is non-existent. The 'collaboration' you promised is actually just you juggling three different versions of the truth. When technical debt outpaces your ability to explain the codebase, your freelance business hits a ceiling.

The Antigravity project template is the antidote to this chaos. By enforcing a rigid, modular file structure and embedding contextual metadata, you transform your codebase into a machine-readable asset. This isn't just about organizing folders; it's about building a scalable foundation that allows Claude Code to jump into any project, understand the context, and execute changes with surgical precision.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Standardize your project structure to ensure all future contributors or AI agents operate on the same architectural pattern.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity modular SaaS template. Enforce strict separation between core logic, UI components, and API handlers.

steps.title: Inject Contextual Metadata
steps.description: Add metadata headers to your base files so the AI understands the business logic behind the code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project structure and inject 'Context-Aware' headers into the top of every core module to explain its purpose and dependencies.

steps.title: Define the Shared Knowledge Base
steps.description: Create a .context file that acts as the 'source of truth' for the project's collaboration rules and API contracts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a .context file that outlines the API contract, database schema, and coding style guide for this project.

steps.title: Configure Claude Code Sync
steps.description: Link your local environment to the Antigravity template to ensure real-time synchronization of project rules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the current repository to sync with the Antigravity ruleset, ensuring all new files follow the established pattern.

steps.title: Execute Collaborative Feature Build
steps.description: Use Claude Code to build a new feature while referencing the Antigravity structure to ensure modularity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a new user authentication module following the existing Antigravity pattern. Ensure it integrates with the shared state manager.

pro_tips: Use a dedicated .antigravity-ignore file to prevent AI agents from hallucinating based on build artifacts or massive node_modules.
Always run 'claude audit' after a major refactor to ensure your code hasn't drifted from the Antigravity structural requirements.
Tag your commits with 'AG-Template' to maintain a clear history for clients who need to audit your build process later.
Create a 'system-prompts.md' file in your root; Claude Code will automatically ingest this to maintain your specific coding voice.
If you're working in a team, share the .context file via Git so every developer's Claude instance shares the same architectural constraints.
resources: Best Practices for AI Engineering: https://ai.engineering/guide
content.next_steps: Once your template is live, run 'claude deploy --preview' to test the integration in a sandbox environment before handing off the repository to your client.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your own codebase and start building with professional-grade infrastructure that makes collaboration effortless. Your clients are waiting for this level of reliability. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
